canonical_definition: Geometry3k is characterized in the provided literature as both a benchmark and a dataset for evaluating reasoning in the domain of plane geometry. The prevailing usage is as an instrument to measure mathematical reasoning and geometry problem solving. One definition describes it as an "early multimodal reasoning benchmark," while another provides more detail on its composition, defining it as a dataset with "human-annotated logical forms." These logical forms are reportedly used to generate "factual QA pairs" for model evaluation. The data shows that Geometry3k is often used in conjunction with other geometry benchmarks like GeoQA, UniGeo, and GEOS. Furthermore, it is sometimes incorporated into larger evaluation suites, as one paper notes that a split of the MathVista benchmark is "collected from... Geometry3K."
papers: Do Large Language Models Truly Understand Geometric Structures?
G-LLaVA: Solving Geometric Problem with Multi-Modal Large Language Model
GeoX: Geometric Problem Solving Through Unified Formalized Vision-Language Pre-training
Is Your Model Really A Good Math Reasoner? Evaluating Mathematical Reasoning with Checklist
Measuring Multimodal Mathematical Reasoning with MATH-Vision Dataset
Visual Sketchpad: Sketching as a Visual Chain of Thought for Multimodal Language Models

definitions.text: A geometry dataset with human-annotated logical forms used to generate factual QA pairs and evaluate geometry problem solving.
